frameTypes: reliable or latest-only by contract profile
headerSections: Parent message ids for causal graph
Policy hash or policy manifest pointer
Shared memory handles needed for decoding
Compact trust metadata for attached payloads

handshakeNegotiation: HELLO and pre-negotiation CAPS must use Version=0.0
reject any other version before negotiation completes
frames must appear in the order HELLO, HELLO, CAPS, CAPS on streams 0, 0, 1, 1
do not admit contract-bound traffic until both CAPS frames are accepted
both HELLO frames must announce the same trustPolicyDigest and both CAPS frames must announce the same acceptedTrustPolicyDigest
fail the session before contract traffic when digests diverge
both CAPS frames must agree on chosenMajor, chosenMinor, chosenProfiles[], offeredCriticalExtensions[], and acceptedCriticalExtensions[]
acceptedTrustPolicyDigest must be the policy digest actually used to validate the peer and bootstrap artifacts
surface the accepted policy digest in audit roots rather than hiding a local policy choice
sessionResumption: resumeTokenBinding
resumed HELLO
resumeTokenBindingDigest must bind the resume token to the prior accepted bootstrap transcript and replay window
reject resumption if the binding cannot be validated
policyDigestPreservation
unknown-peer resume
resumed HELLO and CAPS must converge on the same acceptedTrustPolicyDigest used for the resumed lineage
emit trustPolicyMismatch and require a fresh session when policy digest changes
lineagePreservation
accepted resumption
successful resumption must preserve the prior sessionLineageId
reject resumption and start a fresh lineage if lineage continuity cannot be preserved
newSessionBinding
accepted resumption
the resumed transport session must surface a new sessionId while remaining linked to the prior lineage through accepted resume binding
treat lineage match alone as insufficient for session-bound artifact reuse
replayContinuityRestoration
accepted resumption
dedupe state for unexpired reliable frames must be restored safely
refuse resumption rather than widening the replay window
postResumeTrafficGate
accepted resumption
contract traffic remains blocked until resumed HELLO and CAPS complete under the new protected session
reject resumed contract-bound traffic before handshake completion

basePredicateRuntimeAdmissionProfile.outputEncoding: canonical bytes of schema named by resultSchemaRef

stateTransitionTable: contractRevision is fresh and proposalDigest validates
active candidate revision is stored for audit
counter references a known active candidate and raises contractRevision
countering party may replace prior counterproposal
new counter supersedes prior active candidate and raises contractRevision
previous candidate remains auditable but inactive
accepted digest matches active candidate revision and required signatures validate
declined digest matches active candidate revision
budgets, capability material, and required leases validate as installed
contract is ready and not cancelled
Accepted, Ready, Executing, Delivering, Proved, or Settling

challenge references an accepted subject; if the current state is Accepted or Ready, challengeClass must be compromiseRecoveryFreeze; the current state must be challengeable under disputePolicy; and dispute budget remains
dispute depth increments and blocked-success closure resumes from the challenged subject
repair changes at least one challenged digest and remains within repair-attempt and budget bounds
challenge remains active until a later resolving STATE references the accepted repair outcome
stateTransitionDigest resolves to a StateTransitionRecord whose supporting digests include the accepted Challenge, any accepted Repair, and any required replacement verification result
challenge class closes or is replaced by a new traceable class and the contract resumes from the challenged checkpoint rather than a local default
delivery digest validates against declared schema and permissions
proof binds to accepted delivery subject
evidence bundle becomes part of acceptance trace
acceptance predicate passes, no blocking challenge remains, and final success authority threshold is met
settlement inputs are frozen for accounting
completionMode allows partial completion, no blocking challenge remains, and partial acceptance is machine-evaluable
Accepted, Ready, Executing, Delivering, Proved, or Challenged
frame matches active revision or stronger cancellation policy
stateTransitionDigest resolves to a StateTransitionRecord whose supporting digests include exactly one accepted CloseDecision carrying blockedCloseOutcomeApplied=cancelWithoutRelease

stateTransitionDigest resolves to a StateTransitionRecord whose supporting digests include exactly one accepted FailureEvent; if the current state is Settling and causeCode=policyFailClosed, those supporting digests must instead include exactly one accepted CloseDecision carrying blockedCloseOutcomeApplied=failClosed
stateTransitionDigest resolves to a StateTransitionRecord whose supporting digests include exactly one accepted RollbackStart
RollingBack, Failed, Cancelled, PartiallyCompleted, or Completed
settlement inputs reduce from canonical receipts, accepted result state, and any required finality candidate

close scope is contract, closeTraceDigest resolves to an accepted CloseDecision, finality conditions are satisfied or deterministically mapped through blockedCloseOutcome, and no blocking challenge remains
no further semantic mutation is permitted
trustFinalityBridge: policyRootBinding
trust, bridge, and finality decisions
BootstrapAcceptance.acceptedTrustPolicyDigest and ResumeAcceptance.acceptedTrustPolicyDigest are the session-lineage roots for later bridge, revocation, and finality decisions
treat policy drift as a fresh-lineage event, not as a local override
bridgeProfileRequirement
bridge-mediated verification
requireVerifierBridge is portable only under an accepted VerifierBridgeProfile
do not silently substitute a local bridge, local timeout, or local redaction rule
bridgeSubmissionBinding
bridge-mediated verification
bridgeReplay verification must bind back to one accepted BridgeSubmission and preserve subjectDigest, evaluationScopeDigest, predicateRuntimeProfileDigest, proofProfileDigest, and contractRevision
reject bridge results whose binding tuple diverges
finalityEvidenceBinding
terminal close
CLOSE must bind to the same finality candidate advertised by SETTLE and must reference an accepted CloseDecision through closeTraceDigest
reject close when finality and close-decision digests do not align
blockedCloseDeterminism
blocked or stale finality
blockedCloseOutcome must deterministically map unsatisfied finality into a terminal result
do not leave contracts indefinitely in Settling
staleDomainExclusion
quorum and witness counting
stale, revoked, unresolved, or policy-ineligible domains must be excluded from witness and quorum counts
do not count tainted domains toward positive close
lateEvidenceAuditOnly
post-close evidence
once maxFinalityWaitMs expires and blockedCloseOutcome resolves the revision, later witness, anchor, revocation, or settlement-rail evidence is audit-only
do not reopen Settling or Closed for the same revision
conflictingFinalityReopensDispute
settling and close evidence
conflicting witness or anchor evidence for the same candidate context must move the contract back into dispute before irreversible close
reject silent close on contradictory finality material
